question.text: Who may represent an appellant before the Securities Appellate Tribunal under the Depositories Act?

answer.text: Only officers of the appellant company, not external professionals
The appellant in person, or one or more chartered accountants, company secretaries, cost accountants, legal practitioners, or any of its officers
Only chartered accountants or company secretaries
Only legal practitioners (advocates) enrolled under the Bar Council

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under the Depositories Act, the appellant may either appear in person or authorize one or more chartered accountants or company secretaries or cost accountants or legal practitioners or any of its officers to present his or its case before the SAT. **The Trap:** The SAT allows a wider range of professionals to represent appellants, not just lawyers. CAs, CS, cost accountants, and even the appellant's own officers are permitted. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This provision makes SAT proceedings more accessible compared to regular courts, allowing financial professionals who understand market technicalities to represent parties.
